key_points: A current-carrying conductor produces a magnetic field around it
The direction of magnetic field around a straight conductor can be determined using the right-hand thumb rule
A solenoid is a coil of wire that produces a magnetic field similar to a bar magnet when current flows through it
A current-carrying conductor placed in a magnetic field experiences a force
Electric motors convert electrical energy into mechanical energy
examples: Compass needle deflecting near a current-carrying wire
Electromagnets used in electric bells and cranes
Electric fan and mixer using electric motors
Generators producing electricity in power stations
formulas: Fleming's Left-Hand Rule → Determines direction of force on a current-carrying conductor
Electromagnetic Induction → Production of current due to changing magnetic field
Frequency of AC in India → 50 Hz
exam_tips: Understand the right-hand thumb rule clearly
Practice diagrams of magnetic field around a conductor and solenoid
Learn the working principle of electric motor and generator
Remember the difference between AC and DC
